product.title: Product
product.description: From requirements to production-ready code in minutes.

meta.title: Turn First-pass PRDs into Approved Implementation Plans
meta.description: Symphony turns product intent into a durable, team-reviewed plan that engineers can approve and execute. Stop rebuilding context across handoffs. Ship faster on real, existing codebases.

features.title: The missing step between PRD and build
features.description: Symphony makes Implementation Plans a first-class artifact: generated from your inputs, reviewed by your team, approved with clear ownership, and saved as the system of record.

items.title: Generate the Implementation Plan
items.description: Start from a PRD or a lightweight issue. Symphony generates a structured Implementation Plan using your repo context, so the plan is explicit and ready for review.

items.title: Review and approve, fast
items.description: Assign a final approver, collect edits, and move Draft to Approved. Plans become shared context for humans and agents, not disposable chat output.

items.title: Immutable versions, forever
items.description: Every save creates a preserved version. Keep the full history of PRDs and plans so teams can trace decisions, reuse context, and diagnose failures when output is wrong.

items.title: Right-sized workflows
items.description: Pick a preset workflow for the work at hand. Run lean for quick fixes or add gates for higher stakes work. Human touchpoints are explicit, owned, and tracked.

stats.title: Faster execution, less rework
stats.description: Symphony reduces planning churn by turning ephemeral plans into approved artifacts. Track time-to-approval, revision count, and plan quality so velocity compounds over time.

items.title: Game-changing for our velocity
items.description: We went from PRD to working prototype in hours instead of weeks. The generated code is cleaner than what some of our junior devs write, and it passes our strict code review standards.

items.title: Finally, AI that understands architecture
items.description: This isn't just code generation—it's architectural thinking. The plans are thoughtful, the code is production-ready, and my team actually enjoys working with the output.

items.title: The code quality blew me away
items.description: Type-safe, tested, documented, and following all our conventions. It's like having a senior engineer who never gets tired and always follows best practices.

items.title: Reduced our sprint planning by 80%
items.description: We feed in the PRD and get back a complete technical plan with component breakdowns, API contracts, and even implementation estimates. Our planning meetings are now 30 minutes instead of 3 hours.

faq.title: Common questions
faq.description: Clear answers for teams who want to move fast.

items.question: What makes Symphony different from using an AI chat to plan work?
items.answer: Symphony turns planning into a shared, durable artifact. Plans are saved, versioned, reviewed, and approved as a team. That means less context loss, fewer mismatches with the PRD, and a single source of truth that humans and agents can reuse.

items.question: Can Symphony start from something smaller than a full PRD?
items.answer: Yes. Symphony supports lightweight inputs for small units of work, like bugs and quick fixes. You can start a workflow from an issue-sized artifact, generate the plan automatically, and only add rigor when the work demands it.

items.question: How do approvals work?
items.answer: Artifacts move from Draft to Approved with a clearly assigned final approver. In workflows, stages can be owned by a human or an agent. Human-owned stages pause for review and can be approved, approved with comments, or rejected.

items.question: Do you keep history, or do plans get overwritten?
items.answer: Symphony preserves every version. Each save creates a new immutable revision, so you can always reference prior decisions, reuse past context, and see how the plan evolved as the team learned.

items.question: Who is Symphony for?
items.answer: Teams building on real codebases where context matters. Especially product managers and engineering leads at small companies who want a lightweight way to turn intent into approved plans, then move quickly with confidence.

cta.title: Make the plan the source of truth
cta.description: Stop losing context between product and engineering. Generate a plan, review it quickly, approve it, and move. Symphony makes AI-assisted development work on brownfield codebases because the handoff is explicit, durable, and reusable.
cta.secondaryDescription: Loved by teams building on real codebases
